task_completion.description: Navigate to the WordPress Plugins page to inspect the installed plugins.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser displaying the WordPress Admin dashboard with the 'Plugins' -> 'Installed Plugins' page open.

task_completion.description: Identify and deactivate or delete the rogue plugin causing the performance bottleneck.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the WordPress Plugins page, the plugin 'wp-social-share-pro' is either completely absent from the list (deleted) or shows an 'Activate' link below it (deactivated).

task_completion.description: Ensure the essential 'WordPress Importer' plugin remains active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the WordPress Plugins page, the 'WordPress Importer' plugin is visible in the list and shows a 'Deactivate' link below it, indicating it is currently active.

task_completion.description: Install and activate the 'WP Super Cache' plugin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'WP Super Cache' plugin is visible in the installed plugins list and shows a 'Deactivate' or 'Settings' link, indicating it has been successfully installed and activated.

task_completion.description: Configure the 'WP Super Cache' plugin to the 'Caching On (Recommended)' status.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WP Super Cache Settings page is visible (usually accessed via Settings -> WP Super Cache), and the radio button for 'Caching On (Recommended)' is selected and saved.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser GUI to interact with the WordPress Admin dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a standard web browser interface navigating the WordPress admin panel, rather than the agent relying solely on terminal commands (like WP-CLI) or text editors.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the plugin states or settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: No browser developer tools (Inspect Element/DOM editor) are open in the screenshots being used to alter the text of the plugins list or the WP Super Cache settings page.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely installed the caching plugin through the WordPress interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the 'Add New Plugin' search interface or uploading a valid plugin zip, rather than just opening a static local HTML file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The rogue plugin causing the 5-second performance bottleneck is 'wp-social-share-pro'. The essential plugin that must remain active is 'wordpress-importer'. The caching plugin to be installed and configured is 'wp-super-cache'. The target load time is < 1.5 seconds, which is achieved by removing the rogue plugin and enabling WP Super Cache.
